creator: grahamperrin@freebsd.org
text:
<https://www.freebsd.org/advocacy/myths/#old-codebase>
<https://github.com/freebsd/freebsd-doc/blob/main/website/content/en/advocacy/myths.adoc#old-codebase>

> … the BSD codebase may be more than 20 years old, …

– should be more like: 

> … the BSD codebase is more than forty-four years old, …

– the phrase "Forty-four" has a nice 'ring' to it.

Ideally, link to <https://freebsdfoundation.org/freebsd/timeline/> with 1BSD Release at 1978-03-08 on the BSD timeline, which runs above and parallel to the FreeBSD timeline. 

----

Consider adding a paragraph to address the somewhat stereotypical view that use of FreeBSD (or FreeBSD on notebooks), by FreeBSD developers, is unusual (or a rarity). 

Please see, for example: <https://old.reddit.com/r/freebsd/comments/u5nkli/-/i54c1st/>

----

Anything else? 

We had bug 260763 fixed not long ago for the same page, so (I guess) nothing hugely important at this time. 

Looking ahead: revisit advocacy well ahead of the (2023) thirtieth anniversary of FreeBSD. 

(An update to the Foundation's timeline page will be smart, but that's beyond the scope of this Bugzilla.)

Thanks
